Title: Where the Wild Things Are
Plot summary: After Max wears his wolf suit and makes mischief, he is sent to bed without supper. His room transforms into a jungle, and he sails to an island inhabited by 'Wild Things.' He becomes their king but eventually feels lonely and returns home to find his dinner still hot.
Art Style: Cross-hatched pen and ink with tempera; textured and moody with a vintage feel.
Cover description: A lush, green jungle scene with a horned Wild Thing sleeping near the water. The title is in a bold, hand-drawn serif font in black.
Keywords: monsters, imagination, bedroom, sailing, mischief, king, forest

Title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar
Plot summary: A tiny caterpillar hatches and spends an entire week eating through a variety of foods—from apples to chocolate cake. After getting a stomachache and eating a green leaf, he builds a cocoon and emerges as a beautiful butterfly.
Art Style: Distinctive collage art using hand-painted tissue paper and vibrant colors.
Cover description: A bright green caterpillar with a red head on a clean white background. Title in a playful, rounded sans-serif font.
Keywords: nature, lifecycle, food, butterfly, days of the week, growth

Title: The Cat in the Hat
Characters: The Cat, Sally, Conrad, The Fish, Thing 1, Thing 2
Genre Expectations: You are the witness to chaos
Plot summary: Two children are bored at home on a rainy day while their mother is out. A tall anthropomorphic cat wearing a red-and-white striped hat arrives and creates havoc with various tricks and his companions, Thing 1 and Thing 2, before cleaning up just in time.
Art Style: Simple line drawings with a limited color palette of red, blue, and black.
Cover description: The Cat in a tall striped hat leaning over the title. Background is a vibrant blue. High-contrast primary colors and iconic Dr. Seuss script.
Keywords: rhyme, rain, boredom, mischief, cleanup, house, pets

Title: The Tale of Peter Rabbit
Characters: Peter Rabbit, Mrs. Rabbit, Mr. McGregor, Flopsy, Mopsy, Cotton-tail
Plot summary: Despite his mother's warnings, Peter Rabbit sneaks into Mr. McGregor's garden. He eats too many vegetables and is spotted by the gardener. After a harrowing chase where he loses his clothes, he escapes back to the woods, exhausted and sick.
Art Style: Soft, delicate watercolors with fine detail and naturalistic depictions of animals.
Cover description: Small, centered illustration of Peter Rabbit in his blue jacket on a white or pale background. Elegant, classic serif typography.
Keywords: garden, bunny, vegetables, chase, danger, disobedience, farm

Title: Goodnight Moon
Characters: The Bunny, The Quiet Old Lady
Plot summary: A young bunny tucked in bed says goodnight to all the objects in his 'great green room'—from the red balloon to the three little bears sitting on chairs. The room gradually gets darker as the bunny falls asleep.
Art Style: High-contrast alternating pages of bright, saturated primary colors and black-and-white sketches.
Cover description: A view of the green bedroom with a yellow fireplace and a window showing the moon. Title in thick, blocky yellow font.

Title: Charlotte's Web
Plot summary: Wilbur the pig is the runt of the litter and faces slaughter. A clever spider named Charlotte saves him by weaving praise-filled messages like 'Some Pig' into her webs. The story explores themes of friendship, sacrifice, and the cycle of life.
Art Style: Classic pen-and-ink sketches by Garth Williams; expressive and nostalgic.
Cover description: Wilbur looking up at Charlotte in her web, with Fern leaning on the fence. Pastoral colors, serif font for the title.
Keywords: farm, friendship, spider, pig, barn, loyalty, life cycle

Title: Matilda
Characters: Matilda Wormwood, Miss Honey, Miss Trunchbull, Mr. Wormwood
Plot summary: Matilda is a brilliant girl born to cruel parents. She discovers she has telekinetic powers. At school, she befriends the kind Miss Honey and uses her powers to defeat the tyrannical headmistress, Miss Trunchbull, ultimately finding a new home.
Art Style: Quirky, energetic pen-and-ink illustrations by Quentin Blake.
Cover description: A small girl sitting on a tall stack of books. Bright, colorful background with scratchy, whimsical illustrations.
Keywords: magic, books, school, teacher, cruel parents, intelligence, justice

Title: The Polar Express
Characters: The Boy, The Conductor, Santa Claus
Plot summary: A young boy who doubts the existence of Santa Claus boards a magical train on Christmas Eve bound for the North Pole. After receiving the first gift of Christmas—a silver bell—he learns that the spirit of the holiday remains for those who truly believe.
Art Style: Lush, atmospheric oil pastels with soft focus and glowing light effects.
Cover description: A massive steam engine roaring through a snowy forest at night. Dark deep blues and glowing yellows. Classic elegant font.
Keywords: Christmas, winter, train, North Pole, magic, snow, bell, holiday

Title: The Giving Tree
Genre Expectations: You are the taker / the giver
Plot summary: The story follows the lifelong relationship between a boy and an apple tree. The tree gives the boy her apples, branches, and eventually her trunk to make him happy. By the end, the tree is a stump, and the boy is an old man who just needs a place to sit.
Art Style: Minimalist black-and-white line drawings; very simple and clean.
Cover description: A simple green background with a black line drawing of the tree leaning toward the boy. Title in a simple, modest font.
Keywords: generosity, nature, aging, sacrifice, love, friendship, selfless

Title: Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus!
Plot summary: When a bus driver takes a break, he asks the reader to ensure the pigeon doesn't drive the bus. The pigeon spends the rest of the book using every trick, bribe, and tantrum imaginable to convince the reader to let him behind the wheel.
Art Style: Modern, minimalist cartooning with expressive posing and muted, pastel-like colors.
Cover description: A frantic-looking pigeon against a light blue background. Large, friendly hand-lettered font.
Keywords: humor, interactive, bird, persuasion, funny, transport
